articles.title: Google’s AI search summaries will now quote Reddit
articles.summary: Google's AI Search now includes previews from firsthand sources like Reddit and social media forums, allowing users to find trusted perspectives and real human feedback alongside search results.

articles.title: Microsoft’s Office and LinkedIn chief now runs Teams in latest reshuffle
articles.summary: Microsoft's Ryan Roslansky, who leads LinkedIn and Office, now oversees Teams as head of the new Work Experiences Group in a leadership reshuffle.

articles.title: Chrome’s AI features may be hogging 4GB of your computer storage
articles.summary: Chrome automatically downloads a 4GB AI model file (weights.bin) for Gemini Nano features, consuming significant storage space without user awareness.

articles.title: 3 days left to lock in 50% off a second ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
articles.summary: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 offers 50% off a second ticket until May 8. Purchase one ticket and get another at half price to boost your tech industry visibility.

articles.title: AI boom pushes Samsung to $1T
articles.summary: Samsung reached $1 trillion valuation as shares surged due to AI-driven chip demand, becoming only the second Asian company after TSMC to achieve this milestone.

articles.title: Peter Sarlin’s QuTwo reaches $380M valuation in angel round
articles.summary: Finnish AI lab QyTw0, founded by former AMD Silo AI CEO Peter Sarlin, raised €25 million in angel funding, reaching a €325 million valuation. The funding reflects strong investor interest in European AI and quantum computing ventures.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS. The funding addresses developer frustration with legacy cloud platforms' complexity and cost.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, an open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality for free and runs locally without subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI tokens and a coding challenge that attracted thousands of applicants. The creative hiring stunt decoded to a puzzle about Berlin's Berghain nightclub bouncer algorithm.

articles.title: Ars Asks: Share your shell and show us your tricked-out terminals!
articles.summary: Despite predictions of the GUI's dominance, command-line terminals remain essential tools for many tasks, offering superior efficiency compared to point-and-click interfaces for power users.

articles.title: Widely used Daemon Tools disk app backdoored in monthlong supply-chain attack
articles.summary: Daemon Tools disk mounting application was compromised in a month-long supply-chain attack, distributing malicious updates through official developer servers that infect Windows systems with malware.

articles.title: Why Reddit blocked my daily visit to its mobile website
articles.summary: Reddit blocked mobile web access with a mandatory app overlay, forcing users to download the app to continue browsing. The overlay offers no bypass option, pushing users toward the native application.

articles.title: The Download: seafloor science and military chatbots
articles.summary: New affordable submersibles by Orpheus Ocean could revolutionize deep-sea exploration and mineral mapping at lower costs. However, their use by mining companies raises environmental concerns about seafloor ecosystems.

articles.title: The Download: inside the Musk v. Altman trial, and AI for democracy
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face off in a landmark trial over OpenAI's transition to for-profit status. Musk claims he was misled about the company's business model change.

articles.title: A blueprint for using AI to strengthen democracy
articles.summary: AI is reshaping how societies form beliefs and govern themselves, similar to past information revolutions. Without careful design, it risks straining democratic institutions, but could address civic engagement and polarization.

articles.title: Adding Benchmaxxer Repellant to the Open ASR Leaderboard
articles.summary: The Open ASR Leaderboard adds Benchmaxxer Repellant to prevent gaming metrics through optimization tricks. This ensures fairer evaluation of automatic speech recognition systems.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs represent advances in large language model architecture and training. The article explores the technical foundations and construction methods behind these models.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra is now available as an inference provider on Hugging Face, enabling users to access powerful AI models through their platform integration.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of artificial intelligence, exploring upcoming developments and trends in the AI field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to boost productivity and creativity in content creation. These solutions enable companies to work more efficiently while enhancing imaginative output.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, providing developers with tools to advance agricultural technology and sustainable farming practices through AI and digital innovation.

articles.title: Google is partnering with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on the $3.5 million Future Vision film competition.
articles.summary: Google partners with XPRIZE and Range Media Partners on a $3.5 million Future Vision film competition to showcase innovative storytelling about technology's future impact.

articles.title: The latest AI news we announced in April 2026
articles.summary: Google announced their latest AI updates in April 2026, featuring new capabilities and improvements across their AI product portfolio.

articles.title: Reduce friction and latency for long-running jobs with Webhooks in Gemini API
articles.summary: Google's Gemini API now supports event-driven webhooks, a push-based notification system that reduces friction and latency for long-running jobs by eliminating inefficient polling.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Research explores AI co-clinician development to augment healthcare delivery. The initiative aims to integrate AI assistance into clinical workflows to enhance patient care outcomes.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage frontier artificial intelligence technology for research advancement.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by decoupling local and global updates, improving fault tolerance and reducing communication overhead in large-scale model training.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #340 - OpenAI vs Musk + Microsoft, DeepSeek v4, Vision Banana
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming the company misappropriated charitable assets. The trial revealed evidence about model training practices and settlement discussions between the parties.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #243 - GPT 5.5, DeepSeek V4, AI safety sabotage
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's GPT-5.5 release with improved coding capabilities and higher pricing, xAI's Grok Voice Think Fast 1.0 for real-time voice agents, and concerns about AI safety sabotage.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: The podcast discusses OpenAI's new ChatGPT image model with improved text accuracy and screenshot generation, alongside updates on Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6 models.

articles.title: US government increases AI suppliers and rethinks Anthropic’s role
articles.summary: The US Pentagon expanded its AI supplier roster to include Microsoft, Amazon, Nvidia, and Reflection AI for classified operations, while canceling a $200 million Anthropic contract over disagreements about surveillance and weapons use.

articles.title: Google tests Remy AI agent for Gemini as focus turns to user control
articles.summary: Google is testing Remy, a new AI personal agent for Gemini designed to take actions on users' behalf for work and daily tasks. The tool is currently being tested by Google employees in a staff-only version of the Gemini app.

articles.title: Physical AI raises governance questions for autonomous systems
articles.summary: Physical AI governance faces challenges as autonomous systems move into robots and industrial equipment. Testing, monitoring, and controlling real-world AI interactions becomes critical with robot installations doubling over a decade.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agents cause task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging capabilities for collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How Memory Sparse Attention scales LLM memory to 100 million tokens
articles.summary: Memory Sparse Attention (MSA) enables large language models to process context windows up to 100 million tokens while maintaining accuracy. This breakthrough significantly expands LLM memory capacity.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that Claude Code and similar AI coding assistants are leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, posing significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: I tested 5G across rural America during a 3-day roadtrip - and it didn't go well
articles.summary: A reporter tested 5G coverage across rural America during a three-day road trip and found disappointing results in small towns compared to urban areas and interstates.

articles.title: This useful Pixel phone sensor is one of my favorite tools - but Google may replace it soon
articles.summary: Google's next Pixel phones may replace the thermometer sensor with 'Pixel Glow' LEDs, potentially removing a useful feature that users appreciate.

articles.title: Google's AI Overviews will show you advice from other people now
articles.summary: Google is enhancing AI Overviews with five new features, including improved source previews and exploration capabilities, plus advice from other people.

articles.title: Implementing Statistical Guardrails for Non-Deterministic Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses implementing statistical guardrails for non-deterministic agents, which produce varying outputs from identical inputs. These safeguards ensure reliable and consistent behavior in variable AI systems.

articles.title: Agentic RAG Explained in 3 Levels of Difficulty
articles.summary: Agentic RAG combines retrieval-augmented generation with autonomous agents for advanced AI systems. The article explains the concept across three difficulty levels for different audiences.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, an algorithmic suite for quantization and compression of large language models and vector search engines, essential for RAG systems.

articles.title: Why I Don’t Trust LLMs to Decide When the Weather Changed
articles.summary: A physicist discusses building production-grade AI agents, highlighting concerns about relying on LLMs for critical decisions like weather pattern analysis without proper verification methods.

articles.title: Deconstruct Any Metric with a Few Simple ‘What’ Questions
articles.summary: Dashboards and data storytelling often mislead. The article teaches deconstructing metrics using simple 'what' questions to understand true data meaning.

articles.title: Discrete Time-To-Event Modeling – Predicting When Something Will Happen
articles.summary: An article explaining discrete time-to-event modeling fundamentals, including time discretization, censoring, and life table methods for predicting when events occur.

articles.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
articles.summary: NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet fabric is advanced AI networking technology deployed by OpenAI, Microsoft, and Oracle for building powerful AI infrastructure at scale.

articles.title: NVIDIA and ServiceNow Partner on New Autonomous AI Agents for Enterprises
articles.summary: NVIDIA and ServiceNow partner to develop autonomous AI agents for enterprises, advancing AI from generation and reasoning to autonomous action in real business workflows with safety and control.

articles.title: Nemotron Labs: What OpenClaw Agents Mean for Every Organization
articles.summary: OpenClaw agents are gaining massive developer interest, with over 100,000 GitHub stars by early 2026. The open-source project enables organizations to build specialized AI systems using open models and datasets on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: How Hapag-Lloyd uses Amazon Bedrock to transform customer feedback into actionable insights
articles.summary: Hapag-Lloyd, a leading shipping company, uses Amazon Bedrock to analyze customer feedback and gain actionable insights. This AI implementation helps their digital teams improve customer experience across global operations.

articles.title: Streamlining generative AI development with MLflow v3.10 on Amazon SageMaker AI
articles.summary: Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow Apps now supports MLflow v3.10, introducing enhanced capabilities for generative AI development with improved experiment tracking, observability, and evaluation features for data scientists and ML engineers.

articles.title: Introducing OS Level Actions in Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Browser now supports OS-level actions, enabling AI agents to interact with system dialogs, security prompts, and native elements beyond the DOM that were previously inaccessible.

articles.title: How to Build In-Vehicle AI Agents with NVIDIA: From Cloud to Car
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables building advanced in-vehicle AI agents that transition automotive cockpits from basic rule-based systems to intelligent multimodal AI capable of reasoning and planning.

articles.title: Building for the Rising Complexity of Agentic Systems with Extreme Co-Design
articles.summary: AI is shifting from simple request-response interactions to complex agentic systems where agents autonomously call tools, spawn sub-agents, and manage their own context and memory.

articles.title: Optimize Supply Chain Decision Systems Using NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills
articles.summary: NVIDIA cuOpt Agent Skills optimizes supply chain decisions by automating complex operational research problems traditionally requiring specialized teams to translate business questions into mathematical models.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Research study explores how generational differences influence consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms, conducted in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and making solutions publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background developed innovative XR technology by applying unique perspectives and unconventional approaches to the field.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and understand model decision-making processes. This advances AI transparency and trustworthiness by analyzing feature attribution and model behavior through multiple analytical lenses.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents an information-driven approach to design imaging systems by using an encoder to map objects to images, which are then corrupted by noise. An information estimator quantifies measurement quality using only noisy data and noise models.
